title: Post 1 — The Mother Tongue of English (Proto-Indo-European)

shots.video_prompt: The Archive keeper host speaks directly to camera in English, lip-synced, measured documentary gravitas. Delivers slowly: "Before Latin. Before Greek. Before anything was written down — there was one language. No one ever recorded it. And yet we can rebuild it… from its children." Subtle slow push-in, warm brass lamp on face, hands near open codex on worktable.
shots.speech_text: Before Latin. Before Greek. Before anything was written down — there was one language. No one ever recorded it. And yet we can rebuild it… from its children.

shots.video_prompt: Host push-in medium-close, speaks signature beat in English, lip-synced, thoughtful pause between clauses. Delivers: "What did they actually say? … And how do we prove it?" Still grounded, trustworthy delivery.
shots.speech_text: What did they actually say? … And how do we prove it?

shots.on_screen:
*h₂éwis, h₁éḱwōs h₁éḱontes
[ˈh₂é.wis ˈh₁é.kʷoːs ˈh₁é.kʷon.tes]
RECONSTRUCTED — NOT ATTESTED
shots.video_prompt: The same Archive host now speaks reconstructed Proto-Indo-European slowly and clearly, lip-synced, scholarly documentary tone, never claiming it is a recording. PIE line: "h₂éwis, h₁éḱwōs h₁éḱontes" IPA target [ˈh₂é.wis ˈh₁é.kʷoːs ˈh₁é.kʷon.tes]. On-screen feel: bold RECONSTRUCTED warning tags, asterisk forms visible. Steady medium shot, lamp light, deliberate pace.

shots.on_screen: Comparative method · Latin ovis ← *h₂éwis · Sanskrit ávi- · English ewe
shots.video_prompt: Host back in Archive, gestures toward manuscripts on table while explaining in English, lip-synced. Delivers: "We compare its daughters — Latin, Sanskrit, Old English — and work backward to the sound that must have stood behind them all. That's the comparative method. It's reconstruction, not a recording — and we'll always tell you which is which." Educational hand gesture toward codex, calm authority, honesty about uncertainty.
shots.speech_text: We compare its daughters — Latin, Sanskrit, Old English — and work backward to the sound that must have stood behind them all. That's the comparative method. It's reconstruction, not a recording — and we'll always tell you which is which.

shots.video_prompt: Host warm close, speaks tagline in English, lip-synced. Delivers: "Bring the dead tongues back — one attestation at a time. DAVID." Slight smile of quiet wonder, medium close-up.
shots.speech_text: Bring the dead tongues back — one attestation at a time. DAVID.

provenance_card.title: DAVID · PROVENANCE · Post 1
provenance_card.subtitle: Post 1 — The Mother Tongue of English (Proto-Indo-European)
lines: TEXT [reconstructed]: August Schleicher, Compendium der vergleichenden Grammatik (1861/62); modern recension Schleicher's Fable. Reconstructed — not attested.
PRONUNCIATION [reconstructed]: reconstructed late PIE phonology (laryngeal theory); moderate — scholarly consensus with debated laryngeal detail.
TRANSLATION: The sheep and the horses (were) going…
SOURCES: Wikipedia / Wiktionary (CC BY-SA), Wikidata (CC0), retrieved 2026-06-18.
provenance_card.footer: reconstructed text · reconstructed pronunciation · comparative method
